narration: Define a function readExcel that takes error and data parameters, prints data to console if successful, or logs error if file not found.
Execute fs.readFile('read-excel.js', null, readExcel) to read the file and verify its content is displayed in the console.
Implement fs.writeFile('write.js', 'data saved', writeFileCallback) to create a new file with specific content.
Demonstrate that fs.writeFile overwrites existing file content by writing 'data saved' to write.js.
Describe fs.openFile as an alternative method that appends data to the end of an existing file instead of overwriting it.
Implement fs.openFile('write.js', 'new data', openFileCallback) to demonstrate file appending behavior.
Explain that fs.unlink takes the file path and a callback function to remove a file from the filesystem.
Implement fs.unlink('write.js', unlinkCallback) to delete the previously created file.
